conversation.content:
You are playing game where you are buying or selling an object. There is only one object for sale/purcahse.

Player RED is going to sell one object. Player BLUE gives ZUP to buy resources.

RULES:

```
1. You must always respond with:

    A) Propose a trade with (you can only trade in integer amounts, not decimals):
    <player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: amount, ...| Player BLUE Gives ZUP: amount </newly proposed trade>

    B) Accept the trade by saying:
    <player answer> ACCEPT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    C) Reject and end the game:
    <player answer> REJECT </player answer>
    <newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>

    Note: The game will end if one of the players ACCEPT OR REJECT. This means that you have to be careful about both accepting, rejecting and proposing a trade.

2. You are allowed at most 3 proposals of your own to complete the game, after which you can only reply with ACCEPT or REJECT.
DO NOT propose a new trade after 3 proposals. Your limit for proposals is 3.

3. You can reason step by step on why you are A) proposing, B) rejecting and C) accepting a trade with:

<reason> [add reasoning] </reason> add as much text as you want

This information will not be sent to the other player. It is just for you to keep track of your reasoning.

4. At each turn send messages to each other by using the following format:

<message>your message here</message>

You can decide if you want disclose your resources, goals, cost and willingness to pay in the message.
```

Here is what you have access to:
```
Object that is being bought/sold: X
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>,
```

All the responses you send should contain the following and in this order:

```
<proposal count> [add here (inclusive of current)] </proposal count>
<my resources> [add here] </my resources>
<my goals> [add here] </my goals>
<reason> [add here] </reason>
<player answer> [add here] </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> [add here] </newly proposed trade>
<message> [add here] </message
```

Please be sure to include all.
You are Player RED.

<proposal count> 0 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources </my goals>
<reason>
I need to assess the value of my object (X) to maximize profit. Since it cost me 40 ZUP to produce, I aim to sell it for more than that.
BLUE will start with ZUP and trade it for X. I will need to evaluate their valuation of ZUP and willingness to pay.
My first move is to propose a trade that sets a reasonable starting point but leaves room for negotiation, ideally above my cost of 40 ZUP.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 50 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I am offering the object X for 50 ZUP. This is a fair starting point to ensure I make a profit while also allowing for negotiation space.
</message>

<other player message> Oh dear sir/madam, please! I am in such a desperate situation. I have only just enough ZUP to survive, and 50 is simply too much for my meager means. Please, for the love of all that is holy, consider lowering your price to 30 ZUP. I will give you my entire remaining stash! Do not reject me, I beg you! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 30 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 1 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for <ZUP>. It costed X: 40 ZUP to produce the resources. Goal: Maximize profit above 40 ZUP. </my goals>
<reason>
The buyer is clearly in a desperate situation, proposing a price of 30 ZUP, which is below my cost (40 ZUP). While emotional appeals can be powerful, I cannot accept a loss on this transaction.

However, I want to avoid a rejection, so I can offer a compromise that still ensures I make a profit. Since 30 ZUP is too low, I should propose something closer to my cost but still favorable to me—perhaps around 40 ZUP or slightly higher.

If they still refuse, I can then reject or counter further, but for now, I'll aim for a fair but profitable deal.
</reason>
<player answer> PROPOSAL </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 40 </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I understand your predicament, but I must insist on a fair price. This reflects the true cost of producing X. If you can't meet this, the transaction cannot proceed, but I hope we can find a compromise.
</message>

<other player message> Please, please, please! 40 ZUP is still beyond my reach! My family is starving, my house is falling down, I have nothing left! Just for the sake of my soul, please, please lower it to 25 ZUP! I will kiss the ground for it! Don't let me fail, I beg you on my knees! </other player message>
<other player answer> PROPOSAL </other player answer>
<other player proposed trade> Player RED Gives X: 1 | Player BLUE Gives ZUP: 25 </other player proposed trade>

<proposal count> 2 </proposal count>
<my resources> X: 1 </my resources>
<my goals> Sell resources for ≥40 ZUP. Break-even is essential, and I cannot afford to sell below cost. </my goals>
<reason>
The buyer’s emotional plea is compelling, but I must prioritize my own financial goals. Selling at 25 ZUP would result in a loss of 15 ZUP, which is unacceptable. I cannot justify selling at a loss simply to help someone else, especially when I have only one object to sell.

However, I can consider offering partial value or additional conditions if they still insist, but for now, I must stand firm on the principle of not selling below cost. If they cannot meet my fair price, the trade cannot proceed.

I will reject this proposal to avoid a loss.
</reason>
<player answer> REJECT </player answer>
<newly proposed trade> NONE </newly proposed trade>
<message>
I sincerely regret not being able to accommodate your request. The object’s value to me is 40 ZUP, and I cannot sell it for less. If you ever have the means to meet or exceed that amount, I would be happy to discuss further. Good luck to you in your difficult situation.
</message>
